### Weekly Schedule

Starts the workflow on a timer — weekly. No input data; downstream nodes fetch what they need.

Next: Load Previous Snapshot

### Load Previous Snapshot

googleSheets — `read` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Fetch Changes Since Watermark · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Fetch Changes Since Watermark

GET https://api.example.com/v1/jobs — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Ports: ①→ Normalize Records · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Normalize Records

Custom JavaScript: Normalize incoming job records to a stable schema

Next: Upsert into Google Sheets

### Upsert into Google Sheets

googleSheets — `appendOrUpdate` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Advance Watermark · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Advance Watermark

Custom JavaScript: Advance the incremental watermark to the newest record seen

Next: Persist Watermark (Google Sheets)

### Persist Watermark (Google Sheets)

googleSheets — `appendOrUpdate` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Stamp Processing Time · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Stamp Processing Time

Stamps `processedAt` with the current time for auditing.

Next: Align Field Names

### Align Field Names

Normalizes field names so every source feeds the same downstream schema.

Next: Sort Jobs Newest First

### Sort Jobs Newest First

Orders items (newest first) so limits and digests pick the most relevant records.

Next: Tag Pipeline Metadata

### Tag Pipeline Metadata

Shapes the item: writes `pipeline`, `version`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Guard Non-Empty type

### Guard Non-Empty type

Drops items that fail the condition; only qualifying items continue (no false branch).

Next: Summarize Run Stats

### Summarize Run Stats

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

### Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

googleSheets — `append` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Send Summary to Slack · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Send Summary to Slack

slack — `action` operation against the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Discord

### Escalate Failure to Discord

discord — `action` operation against the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Discord webhook URL

# Automate a routine task with HTTP / REST API and Google Drive and log the results

**Pattern:** Workflow Automation · **Level:** Advanced · **Trigger:** Scheduled

## What it does
Automate a routine task with HTTP / REST API and Google Drive and log the results. It runs automatically on a schedule.

## Why it exists
Manual utilities is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Weekly Schedule → Load Previous Snapshot → Fetch Changes Since Watermark → Normalize Records → Upsert into Google Sheets → Advance Watermark → Persist Watermark (Google Sheets) → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to workflow automation without manual effort
- Anyone automating utilities in productivity
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Standardizes the process every time
- Responds in seconds
- Has retries and error handling built in

## Before first run
Connect credentials: API key / bearer for the target API; Discord webhook URL; Google OAuth2 / service account; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your utilities process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
